3120	
3121	  Embed \(\mathcal{F} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{I}\) into an injective
3122	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\). By
3123	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} the injective \(\mathcal{I}\) has
3124	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and every cover, in
3125	  particular for every \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\). Put \(\mathcal{Q} =
3126	  \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\), giving the short exact sequence
3127	  \[
3128	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0.
3129	  \]
3130	  For each \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are cofinal among
3131	  coverings of \(U\) (condition (2)) and \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
3132	  0\) for them, so Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1} makes the sequence exact on sections,
3133	  \[
3134	    0 \to \mathcal{F}(U) \to \mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U) \to 0,
3135	    \qquad U \in \mathcal{B}.
3136	  \]
3137	  Since by condition (1) every term of a {\v C}ech complex for a cover
3138	  \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\) is a product of values over elements of
3139	  \(\mathcal{B}\), this section-level exactness yields a short exact sequence of
3140	  {\v C}ech complexes
3141	  \[
3142	    0 \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \to
3143	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) \to
3144	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{Q}) \to 0,
3145	  \]
3146	  and hence a long exact sequence of {\v C}ech cohomology. As
3147	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) =
3148	  0\) for \(p > 0\), the long exact sequence forces \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U},
3149	  \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\): the quotient \(\mathcal{Q}\) again has
3150	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\).
3151	
3152	  Now run the sheaf-cohomology long exact sequence of \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to
3153	  \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) over a fixed \(U \in \mathcal{B}\). Under the Ext
3154	  realization of Definition~\ref{def:absolute_cohomology} this is the covariant Ext long
3155	  exact sequence at fixed first argument \(\mathcal{O}_U\)
3156	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}). The injective \(\mathcal{I}\), placed in
3157	  the second Ext argument, has \(H^n(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(n > 0\) by injective
3158	  vanishing (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}). The
3159	  section-level surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(U) = H^0(U, \mathcal{I}) \to H^0(U,
3160	  \mathcal{Q}) = \mathcal{Q}(U)\) established above makes the connecting map into
3161	  \(H^1(U, \mathcal{F})\) zero, whence \(H^1(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). Applying the
3162	  same conclusion to \(\mathcal{Q}\) (another module with vanishing higher {\v C}ech
3163	  cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\)) gives \(H^1(U, \mathcal{Q}) = 0\), and then the
3164	  long exact sequence with \(H^1(U, \mathcal{I}) = H^2(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) forces
3165	  \(H^2(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). Iterating the dimension shift — at each stage
3166	  \(\mathcal{Q}\) plays the role of \(\mathcal{F}\) — gives \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) =
3167	  0\) for all \(p > 0\) and every \(U \in \mathcal{B}\).
3168	
3169	  The argument never uses affine sheaf-cohomology vanishing
3170	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}); its only {\v C}ech-vanishing input is
3171	  condition (3), which on the affine instantiation is supplied by the standard-cover
3172	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. Thus the dependency
3173	  \(\text{affine Serre vanishing} \to \text{this lemma}\) is one-directional and the
3174	  graph is acyclic.
3175	\end{proof}

3177	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
3178	
3179	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
3180	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
3181	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
3182	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
3183	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
3184	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.
3185	
3186	\begin{lemma}
3187	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
3188	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
3189	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
3190	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
3191	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
3192	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
3193	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
3194	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
3195	  % \begin{equation}
3196	  % \label{equation-extended}
3197	  % 0 \to
3198	  % M \to
3199	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
3200	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
3201	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
3202	  % \ldots
3203	  % \end{equation}
3204	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
3205	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
3206	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
3207	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
3208	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
3209	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
3210	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
3211	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
3212	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
3213	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
3214	  \[
3215	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
3216	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
3217	    \qquad
3218	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
3219	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
3220	  \]
3221	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
3222	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
3223	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
3224	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
3225	\end{lemma}
3226	
3227	\begin{proof}
3228	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
3229	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
3230	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
3231	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
3232	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
3233	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
3234	  localisations
3235	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to
3236	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
3237	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
3238	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
3239	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).
3240	\end{proof}
3241	
3242	\begin{lemma}
3243	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
3244	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3245	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
3246	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
3247	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
3248	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
3249	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
3250	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
3251	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
3252	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
3253	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
3254	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
3255	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
3256	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
3257	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
3258	  \[
3259	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
3260	      \;\cong\;
3261	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
3262	  \]
3263	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
3264	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
3265	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
3266	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
3267	\end{lemma}
3268	
3269	\begin{proof}
3270	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
3271	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
3272	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
3273	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
3274	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
3275	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
3276	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
3277	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
3278	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
3279	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
3280	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
3281	  additive homology-preserving functor.
3282	\end{proof}

3284	\begin{lemma}
3285	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
3286	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
3287	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology,
3288	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex}
3289	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3290	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
3291	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
3292	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
3293	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
3294	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
3295	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3296	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
3297	  % associated to the presheaves
3298	  % $$
3299	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
3300	  % $$
3301	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
3302	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
3303	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
3304	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
3305	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
3306	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
3307	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
3308	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
3309	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3310	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
3311	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
3312	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
3313	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
3314	  \[
3315	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
3316	      \;\cong\;
3317	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
3318	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
3319	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
3320	  \]
3321	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
3322	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
3323	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
3324	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
3325	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
3326	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
3327	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
3328	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
3329	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
3330	
3331	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
3332	  of the preimage,
3333	  \[
3334	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
3335	      \;=\;
3336	    H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
3337	  \]
3338	  which uses that \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is again injective over
3339	  \(f^{-1}(V)\), is supplied at point of use; this lemma records the
3340	  resolution-internal sheafify-of-objectwise-homology form.
3341	\end{lemma}
3342	
3343	\begin{proof}
3344	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3345	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3346	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
3347	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
3348	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
3349	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in
3350	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level)
3351	  homology, because the sheafification/inclusion adjunction is exact (it preserves the
3352	  finite limits and colimits computing kernels and cokernels). Hence
3353	  \[
3354	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
3355	      \;\cong\;
3356	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
3357	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
3358	  \]
3359	  which is the stated isomorphism. Since the sheafification of a presheaf vanishes
3360	  exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens, this yields the
3361	  affine-local vanishing criterion.
3362	\end{proof}

3364	\begin{lemma}
3365	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
3366	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3367	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
3368	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
3369	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
3370	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
3371	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
3372	  % L180--199).
3373	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
3374	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3375	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
3376	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
3377	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
3378	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
3379	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
3380	  \to S\). Then:
3381	  \begin{enumerate}
3382	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
3383	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
3384	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
3385	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
3386	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
3387	      \(k \geq 0\),
3388	      \[
3389	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
3390	      \]
3391	  \end{enumerate}
3392	\end{lemma}
3393	
3394	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
3395	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
3396	% the sheaf
3397	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
3398	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
3399	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
3400	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
3401	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
3402	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
3403	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
3404	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
3405	\begin{proof}
3406	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
3407	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
3408	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
3409	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
3410	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
3411	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
3412	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
3413	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
3414	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
3415	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
3416	
3417	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3418	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
3419	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
3420	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
3421	
3422	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
3423	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
3424	  sheafification of
3425	  \[
3426	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
3427	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
3428	  \]
3429	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
3430	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
3431	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
3432	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
3433	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
3434	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
3435	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
3436	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
3437	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
3438	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
3439	
3440	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
3441	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
3442	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
3443	  for every \(k\),
3444	  \[
3445	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
3446	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
3447	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
3448	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
3449	  \]
3450	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
3451	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
3452	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
3453	\end{proof}

3455	\begin{lemma}
3456	[Each {\v C}ech term is $f_*$-acyclic]
3457	  \label{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}
3458	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic}
3459	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3460	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma

3595	  right-derivable additive functor \(G = f_*\)

3597	  \(A = \mathcal{F}\), and the resolution \(J^\bullet = \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) given by

3599	  abstract lemma are exactly the geometric sub-lemmas established above:
3600	  \begin{enumerate}

3602	      complex \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots\) is

3604	      \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
3605	    \item By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic} each term
3606	      \(\mathcal{C}^p\) is right \(f_*\)-acyclic:
3607	      \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
3608	  \end{enumerate}

3610	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th derived functor evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\) and
3611	  the \(i\)-th cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\) to the
3612	  resolution,

3614	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})
3615	      \;\cong\;
3616	    H^i\bigl(f_*\,\mathcal{C}^\bullet\bigr).

3619	  \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) is precisely the relative {\v C}ech complex
3620	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so its \(i\)-th
3621	  cohomology is \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})
3622	  \bigr)\); and \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})\) is the
3623	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) of

# Strategy

## Goal

Prove `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` (`lem:cech_computes_cohomology`),
the protected, frozen-signature target: for `f : X ⟶ S` separated and quasi-compact, `F`
quasi-coherent, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X`, an isomorphism in the weak
existence form `Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F)` (under
`[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]`), where `higherDirectImage f i F =
((pushforward f).rightDerived i).obj F`. End-state: zero inline `sorry` in the cone, zero
project axioms, kernel-only axioms. Extraction from the Algebraic-Jacobian challenge; the
downstream Picard/Quot machinery is out of scope and was carved away.

## Routes

### Route A — acyclic-resolution comparison (CHOSEN)
The augmented Čech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` on `X` (`Cᵖ = ∏ (j_s)_*(F|_{U_s})`) is
(i) a resolution of `F` and (ii) termwise `(pushforward f)`-acyclic. The P4 abstract lemma
"a `G`-acyclic resolution computes `G.rightDerived`" then gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱf_* F`
directly — ONE abstract lemma, NO spectral sequences. The standard Cartan–Leray acyclic-cover
existence proof. Its acyclicity input (ii) reduces to affine Serre vanishing
`H^q(affine, qcoh)=0`, which is NOT free: see the bridge below.

### Route B — two spectral sequences (REJECTED, fallback only)
Čech-to-derived + Leray spectral sequences for `Scheme.Modules`. Rejected: both absent from
Mathlib (multi-thousand-LOC), and Leray needs quasi-coherence of `R^q f_* F`. Strictly heavier
than Route A for the same `Nonempty (…≅…)` goal. NOTE: Route B rests on the SAME irreducible
brick `injective_cech_acyclic` as Route A — rejecting B does not escape it. Fallback only.

### The acyclicity bridge (load-bearing, CORRECTED)
Route A's term-/relative-acyclicity inputs and the general-cover intersection vanishing all
reduce to affine Serre vanishing `H^q(Spec A, qcoh)=0` (Stacks 02KG). This is NOT obtainable
from the P3 contracting homotopy alone: the homotopy proves the Čech *complex* is exact (a
resolution), but term `G`-acyclicity is itself affine vanishing on a smaller affine — a circular
regress with no inductive base. The honest route (Stacks, torsor-free) is the minimal
Čech↔derived bridge P3b: (1) injective `O_X`-modules are Čech-acyclic
(`injective_cech_acyclic`); (2) `ses_cech_h1`; (3) the dimension-shift `cech_vanish_basis` (01EO)
consuming the standard-cover Čech vanishing of P3 as its condition (3). This yields
`affine_serre_vanishing` legitimately, breaking the cycle: P3 produces standard-cover Čech
vanishing; P3b lifts it to affine sheaf vanishing without ever using affine vanishing as an input.

**Direct route for (1)**: `injective_cech_acyclic` does NOT
need presheaf enough-injectives or the δ-functor universality. Two aligned parts: (a) injective
sheaf ⟹ injective presheaf via `Injective.injective_of_adjoint` applied to the
`Mod ↪ PMod` right adjoint (`sheafificationAdjunction`); (b) the free-presheaf complex
`K(𝒰)_• = ⨁ free(yoneda U_…)` resolves `O_𝒰` (objectwise contracting homotopy) and
`Hom(K_•,F) = Č•(𝒰,F)` (section complex) via `freeAdjunction`+Yoneda — so for injective `I`,
`Hom(-,I)` exact gives positive Čech vanishing. This BYPASSES the two expensive bricks
(`presheafModules_enoughInjectives`, `cech_delta_functor_presheaves` — both off the critical path,
dropped from the blueprint). The free-presheaf complex uses Mathlib's `PresheafOfModules.free`, NOT
a bespoke `j_!`. The FULL Stacks-01EO bootstrap (torsor `lemma-cech-h1`, `lemma-kill-cohomology-class`)
remains avoidable; only the injective-acyclicity brick is irreducible.

2790	The two vanishing lemmas below speak about the \emph{absolute} sheaf cohomology
2791	\(H^p(U, \mathcal{F})\) of an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module over an open \(U\). Their
2792	dimension-shift proof needs three structural facts about \(H^p(U, -)\): a degree-zero
2793	identification with global sections, vanishing on injective modules, and a long exact
2794	sequence attached to a short exact sequence of modules. We realize \(H^p(U, -)\) as the
2795	Ext of the structure sheaf, where Mathlib supplies all three facts off the shelf; the
2796	following definition fixes that realization, and the Mathlib dependency anchors after it
2797	pin the exact results it rests on.
2798	
2799	\begin{definition}
2800	[Absolute sheaf cohomology as Ext of the structure sheaf]
2801	  \label{def:absolute_cohomology}
2802	  \uses{lem:ext_bifunctor_mathlib, lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib,
2803	    lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib, lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib,
2804	    lem:hasext_standard_mathlib, lem:modules_restrict_functor_mathlib}
2805	  \textit{Realization of absolute sheaf cohomology in the module-sheaf category.}
2806	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(U \subseteq
2807	  X\) an open subscheme with open immersion \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\). Write
2808	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U\) for the restriction of \(\mathcal{F}\) along \(j\) — the image of
2809	  \(\mathcal{F}\) under the Mathlib restriction functor \(j^\ast :
2810	  X.\mathrm{Modules} \to U.\mathrm{Modules}\) of
2811	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_restrict_functor_mathlib} — and \(\mathcal{O}_U\) for the
2812	  structure sheaf of \(U\) regarded as a module over itself. The \emph{absolute sheaf
2813	  cohomology of \(\mathcal{F}\) over \(U\)} is defined to be the Ext of the structure
2814	  sheaf in the abelian category \(U.\mathrm{Modules} = \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_U)\) of
2815	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules:
2816	  \[
2817	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) \;:=\; \operatorname{Ext}^p_{\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_U)}
2818	      \bigl(\mathcal{O}_U,\; \mathcal{F}|_U\bigr),
2819	    \qquad p \geq 0,
2820	  \]
2821	  equivalently \(\operatorname{Ext}^p_{\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U,
2822	  \mathcal{F})\). Here \(\operatorname{Ext}\) is Mathlib's Ext bifunctor
2823	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_bifunctor_mathlib}) on the abelian category
2824	  \(U.\mathrm{Modules}\); the \(\mathrm{HasExt}\) structure it requires is available
2825	  unconditionally by Lemma~\ref{lem:hasext_standard_mathlib}. The three structural facts
2826	  the downstream dimension-shift arguments consume are then exactly the corresponding
2827	  Mathlib results:
2828	  \begin{enumerate}
2829	  \item \textbf{Degree zero is global sections.} In degree \(0\), Ext is the Hom-group,
2830	    \(\operatorname{Ext}^0(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}|_U) \cong
2831	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}|_U) = \Gamma(U,
2832	    \mathcal{F})\), by the degree-zero Ext comparison
2833	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}); so \(H^0(U, \mathcal{F}) \cong
2834	    \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\).
2835	  \item \textbf{Injective vanishing.} When the \emph{second} Ext argument is an
2836	    injective module \(\mathcal{J}\), one has \(\operatorname{Ext}^{n+1}(-,
2837	    \mathcal{J}) = 0\) by Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}. Placing an
2838	    injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\) in that second-argument role
2839	    (via the \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\) form over \(\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)) yields
2840	    \(H^{n+1}(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) directly, with no appeal to restriction preserving
2841	    injectives.
2842	  \item \textbf{Long exact sequence.} For a short exact sequence \(0 \to \mathcal{F}
2843	    \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) of modules and the \emph{fixed} first
2844	    argument \(\mathcal{O}_U\), the covariant Ext long exact sequence
2845	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}) is the long exact sequence of the
2846	    groups \(H^p(U, -)\), with connecting maps given by composition with the Ext class
2847	    of the extension.
2848	  \end{enumerate}
2849	\end{definition}

#### HARD GATE: `lem:injective_cech_acyclic` (~L2583)

**GATE CLEARS — complete + correct + formalize-ready.**

Detailed assessment:

- **Statement**: Faithful to Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`. Ringed-space hypothesis, injective O_X-module `ℐ`, arbitrary open covering `𝒰`. Conclusion: Čech cohomology concentrated in degree zero (`Ȟ^0=ℐ(U)`, `Ȟ^p=0` for `p>0`). Source-quoted verbatim from Stacks.

- **`\uses{}` completeness**: 9 deps listed — `def:cech_complex`, `def:section_cech_complex`, `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`, `lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`, `lem:injective_of_adjoint`, `lem:mod_pmod_adjunction`, `lem:cech_complex_op_identification`, `lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso`, `lem:hom_into_injective_exact`. Both gate dependencies carry `\leanok`:
  - `lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso` — `\leanok` present (FreePresheafComplex iter-022)
  - `lem:ses_cech_h1` — `\leanok` present (CechBridge iter-024)

- **Proof sketch**: Two explicit parts plus categorical-assembly paragraph.
  - *Part 1*: Injective sheaf → injective presheaf, via `lem:mod_pmod_adjunction` + `lem:injective_of_adjoint` (sheafification is exact left adjoint). Named Lean identifiers cited: `sheafificationAdjunction`, `Injective.injective_of_adjoint`.
  - *Part 2*: Free-complex resolution + Hom-exact. `K(𝒰)_•` resolves `O_𝒰` by `lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`; applying the exact functor `Hom(-,ℐ)` (Part 1) to the augmented exact complex, and using `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`, yields the Čech complex exact in positive degrees.
  - *Categorical assembly*: Op-transport via `HomologicalComplex.op`; `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (from `lem:hom_into_injective_exact`); transported across `homCechComplexMapOpIso` (`lem:cech_complex_op_identification`) and `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso` (`lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso`). The assembly avoids `presheafModules_enoughInjectives` and the δ-functor route — consistent with the direct-route directive in STRATEGY.md.

- **`\lean{}` hints**: `AlgebraicGeometry.injective_cech_acyclic, AlgebraicGeometry.injective_toPresheafOfModules`. Names are plausible given the file split (CechBridge.lean); no local-search conflict.

- **Note (informational)**: `lem:injective_cech_acyclic` has no `\leanok` yet — correct; the sync_leanok phase sets that after the prover lands the declaration.

| anchor label | `\lean{}` name | verified location |
|---|---|---|
| `lem:ext_bifunctor_mathlib` | `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext` | `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` |
| `lem:hasext_standard_mathlib` | `CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard` | `…/Ext/Basic.lean` |

| `lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib` | `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence_exact, covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` | `…/Ext/ExactSequences.lean` |
| `lem:modules_restrict_functor_mathlib` | `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor` | `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean` |

Specifically regarding `lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib`: the blueprint claims "requires only the second argument to be injective". Verified correct — the Lean signature is `[HasExt.{w} C] {X I : C} {n : ℕ} [Injective I]` with no `HasEnoughInjectives` constraint. The `HasExt` structure is unconditionally available from `HasExt.standard`. The "soon" concern about `HasEnoughInjectives` is **dismissed**.

`\uses{def:absolute_cohomology}` is correctly wired into `lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis` and `lem:affine_serre_vanishing`. The chain `lem:injective_cech_acyclic → lem:ses_cech_h1 → lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis → lem:affine_serre_vanishing` (Stacks 01EO → 02KG) is complete and non-circular (`def:absolute_cohomology` uses Ext-based cohomology; `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` does not depend on `affine_serre_vanishing`).

## Severity summary

**R1 — `\restriction` undefined macro** (`Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`, L2935, inside `lem:modules_restrict_functor_mathlib`)

The blueprint-doctor reports `\restriction` as `undefined-macro`: it appears in inline math but is not defined in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`, `macros/print.tex`, or `macros/web.tex`. Standard LaTeX `amssymb` does provide `\restriction` as `\upharpoonright`, so the compiled PDF may render; however the doctor's project-scope macro check flags it and it will cause failures in CI pipeline macro validation.

**Fix**: Add to `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`:
```latex
\providecommand{\restriction}{\upharpoonright}
```
or replace the one occurrence at L2935 directly with `\upharpoonright` or `|_`.

Both gate dependencies (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`, `lem:ses_cech_h1`) have `\leanok`; the proof sketch is detailed enough to guide op-transport assembly in Lean.
